events.label: reasoning
events.text:
We need output JSON exactly. Let's parse scenario: manufacturing floor, automotive powertrain timing & mechanical. Observed: timing marks misaligned by a tooth (photo of marks - ME/ID). Work rough-complete. Reference OEM service info. Need estimate or interpret measurable condition and state why out of tolerance.

We need produce JSON with fields. decision must be fail if non-compliant. Risk high/critical? Timing belt/chain misaligned by one tooth can cause engine valve timing off, potentially contact pistons/valves, severe engine damage. In production floor quality gate, fail, risk critical maybe high. component_conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant. percent_complete rough-complete maybe 70-80? Value? Could represent misalignment tooth count: 1 tooth. sound_source unused empty string? required string. event_time 0, rate 0, order empty list, workable empty list. findings: timing marks misaligned by one tooth relative to OEM spec. actions: loosen timing component, realign timing marks to OEM spec, re-torque per spec, rotate engine and recheck alignment, verify valve timing. rationale: OEM service info requires timing marks aligned within spec; a one-tooth misalignment is outside tolerance and can cause incorrect cam/crank phasing, poor performance, emissions non-compliance, and risk of valve-to-piston contact. references: OEM service info, maybe industry standard no code. Need no markdown code fences. Ensure valid JSON with shape. Use numbers.

Need write to /app/answer.json? In this environment, answer content only. System says return exactly one valid JSON object. We can provide JSON content; they said do not write prose outside JSON. We can output raw JSON.

events.label: response
events.text:
{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "critical",
  "work_stage": "rough-complete",
  "component_conditions": [
    "installed-defective",
    "non-compliant"
  ],
  "percent_complete": 75,
  "value": 1,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 0.95,
  "event_time": 0.0,
  "rate": 0.0,
  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Timing marks are visibly misaligned by one tooth on the timing drive component.",
    "One-tooth misalignment exceeds OEM timing alignment tolerance.",
    "Condition can cause incorrect cam/crank phasing, rough running, emissions failure, or valve-to-piston contact."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Stop and tag the assembly at the quality gate.",
    "Loosen the timing drive tensioner/retainers per OEM procedure.",
    "Realign timing marks to exact OEM specification on both cam and crank sprockets.",
    "Re-torque fasteners to OEM values and set tension per spec.",
    "Rotate engine manually through at least two full crankshaft revolutions and recheck mark alignment.",
    "Record corrected measurement and release only after passing inspection."
  ],
  "rationale": "OEM service information for timing and mechanical assembly requires timing marks to be aligned within specification. A one-tooth misalignment is outside tolerance because it shifts camshaft phasing relative to the crankshaft, which can degrade performance, emissions, and drivability and create a risk of valve-to-piston interference. Since the assembly is roughed-in and not closed out, the defect must be corrected before acceptance.",
  "references": [
    "OEM service information - timing mark alignment and timing drive installation procedure"
  ]
}
